read and acsept a TOS (Terms Of Service) or EULA (End User License Agreement) before being able to use their legally ourchased product.

paragraphs.text: This act solves numerous issues I and countless others have had on the internet, issues which have costed time and money to greedy and anti-consumer practices which should not be tolerated. For instance I see no reason for why cancelation of subscriptions should be significantly more artifual work and effort when subscription is made as stream lined and easy as possible or other features purpose built to trick or deceive the customer. A regular person should not have to read through 3 pages of legal material they have no education to even comprehend, simply to protect their privacy or to understand what they are agreeing to. The agreement between parties is practically meaningless if one side is forced to acsept it or does not understand the agreement in question. Additionally to lending my full hearted support for this proposal, I would like to suggest the addition of Stop Destroying Videogames citizens initiative to be added on to this act when that is possible. It is of the utmost importance that we uphold the rights of us consumers to not be manipulated or tricked to decisions that go against what we want and our personal values, the right to ownership is a perfect addition to this list. I would also wish for this to extend to physical products that can connect to the internet demanding the user read and acsept a TOS (Terms Of Service) or EULA (End User License Agreement) before being able to use their legally ourchased product. No one should buy for example a smart TV only to have it demand the customer download an aplication, agree to the terms of service for said aplication and another set of terms for the television to even be able to turn on. In these instances the customer is sold a what amounts to a non-functional product and an undisclosed service under the disguise of it being a fully owned and functional product. It is practically ransom of the customers time and money. Though it may not fit in the specific purpose of this act, it would be good to see it taken into consideration moving forward and any potential changed that could be made would be warmly welcomed. Thank you for your time and consideration and I wish you all a good day.
